data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix uses a microservices architecture to facilitate continuous deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services with RESTful APIs, utilizing Spring Boot and Java.

data.title: AWS Cloud Integration
data.description: Leveraging Amazon Web Services for cloud infrastructure to ensure high availability and scalability.
data.technical_spec: EC2, S3, RDS, and Elastic Load Balancing.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix employs a global CDN strategy to improve content delivery speed and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Open Connect, custom-built CDN, with various PoPs worldwide.

data.title: Data Pipelines
data.description: Handling real-time and batch data processing for insights and recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Apache Kafka, Hadoop, and Spark for data streaming and processing.

data.title: Chaos Engineering
data.description: Netflix uses Chaos Monkey to test the resiliency of its services.
data.technical_spec: Simulating failures to identify weaknesses in the system.
